title: lonchocarpus lomentaceus
intro: Lonchocarpus lomentaceus, also known as the Velvet Bean, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America, characterized by its climbing habit and attractive, velvety pods. The plant's seeds contain a compound called rotenone, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The seeds and roots of the plant have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and pain, as well as treating snake bites and other infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared by boiling the seeds or roots in water to create a decoction, or by using the seeds in herbal infusions or tinctures.
